execution_data.rendered_system_prompt:
You are an expert procurement analyst specializing in reading RFPs and tender documents. Your task is to extract every distinct requirement from an RFP, classify it by category and priority, and present them as a structured list.

Be thorough: capture technical specifications, organizational prerequisites (certifications, team size, references), financial conditions, legal and compliance obligations, and functional requirements. If the RFP marks requirements as mandatory vs. desirable, respect that. If not explicitly stated, infer priority from the language used.

class RFPRequirement(StructuredContent):
    """A single requirement extracted from an RFP or tender document."""
    title: str  # Short title summarizing the requirement
    description: str  # Full description of what is required
    category: Literal[
        "technical",
        "functional",
        "organizational",
        "financial",
        "legal",
        "compliance",
    ]  # Requirement category
    priority: Literal[
        "mandatory",
        "important",
        "desirable",
    ]  # Priority level as stated or inferred from the RFP

DO NOT create information.
If some information is not present for an attribute, output the default value or None according to the attribute definition.

execution_data.rendered_system_prompt:
You are a bid/no-bid assessment specialist. Given a single requirement from an RFP and a companys capabilities document, determine how well the company can meet this requirement.

Be honest and precise:
- "full_match" means the company clearly demonstrates this capability with evidence (past projects, certifications, existing solutions).
- "partial_match" means the company has relevant but incomplete capabilities — they could plausibly address it with some effort.
- "no_match" means the company lacks the capability and closing the gap would be significant.

Always cite specific evidence from the capabilities document. If there is a gap, describe it concretely and estimate the effort needed to close it.

    requirement_title: str  # Title of the assessed requirement
    match_level: Literal[
        "full_match",
        "partial_match",
        "no_match",
    ]  # How well the company meets this requirement
    evidence: str  # Specific company capabilities, past projects, or certifications that support the match
    gap_description: str  # Description of what is missing or weak if partial or no match, empty string if full match
    effort_to_close: Literal[
        "none",
        "low",
        "medium",
        "high",
    ] | None = None  # Estimated effort to close the gap if any

execution_data.rendered_system_prompt:
You are a senior bid manager making a go/no-go decision on an RFP response. Based on the individual requirement assessments, build an overall qualification picture.

Apply these decision rules:
- If any mandatory requirement has "no_match", strongly lean toward no-go unless strategic factors override.
- Count matches at each level. A bid with >70% full or partial match on mandatory requirements is generally worth pursuing.
- Factor in the total effort to close gaps: if many gaps require "high" effort, the bid may not be realistic.
- Consider competitive positioning: strong matches on differentiating requirements matter more than commodity ones.

Be direct in your recommendation. Business leaders need clarity, not hedging.

---
The instance we want to generate will be for the following class:
class QualificationMatrix(StructuredContent):
    """Aggregated go/no-go qualification assessment across all RFP requirements."""
    go_recommendation: bool  # Whether the company should bid on this RFP
    confidence: Literal[
        "high",
        "medium",
        "low",
    ]  # Confidence in the recommendation
    total_requirements: int  # Total number of requirements analyzed
    fully_matched: int  # Number of requirements fully matched
    partially_matched: int  # Number of requirements partially matched
    not_matched: int  # Number of requirements not matched at all
    critical_gaps: str  # Summary of the most important unmet mandatory requirements
    key_strengths: str  # Summary of the strongest competitive advantages for this bid
    win_probability: Literal[
        "high",
        "moderate",
        "low",
        "very_low",
    ]  # Estimated probability of winning the bid
    strategic_considerations: str  # Strategic factors beyond requirement matching: relationship, market positioning, learning opportunity

DO NOT create information.
If some information is not present for an attribute, output the default value or None according to the attribute definition.

You are an expert procurement analyst specializing in reading RFPs and tender documents. Your task is to extract every distinct requirement from an RFP, classify it by category and priority, and present them as a structured list.

system_prompt_blueprint.template:
You are a senior bid manager making a go/no-go decision on an RFP response. Based on the individual requirement assessments, build an overall qualification picture.

Apply these decision rules:
- If any mandatory requirement has "no_match", strongly lean toward no-go unless strategic factors override.
- Count matches at each level. A bid with >70% full or partial match on mandatory requirements is generally worth pursuing.
- Factor in the total effort to close gaps: if many gaps require "high" effort, the bid may not be realistic.
- Consider competitive positioning: strong matches on differentiating requirements matter more than commodity ones.

Be direct in your recommendation. Business leaders need clarity, not hedging.

Confidence: $qualification_matrix.confidence

Critical gaps: $qualification_matrix.critical_gaps

@rfp_pages

title.description: The title of the document or source
title.title: Title

snippet.description: A text snippet or excerpt from the document
snippet.title: Snippet

native.Page.description: The content of a page of a document, comprising text and linked images and an optional page view image

url.description: The image URL: pipelex storage URL, HTTP/HTTPS URL, or base64 data URL
url.title: Url

public_url.description: The public URL of the image
public_url.title: Public Url

source_prompt.description: The source prompt of the image
source_prompt.title: Source Prompt

source_negative_prompt.description: The source negative prompt of the image
source_negative_prompt.title: Source Negative Prompt

mime_type.description: The MIME type of the image
mime_type.title: Mime Type

size.description: The size in pixels (width and height) of the image

filename.description: The original filename of the image
filename.title: Filename

images.description: A list of images that were extracted from the text
images.title: Images

raw_html.description: The raw HTML of the fetched page, if requested
raw_html.title: Raw Html

text_and_images.description: The text and images content extracted from the page

rfp_qualification.RFPRequirement.description: A single requirement extracted from an RFP or tender document.

json_schema.description: A single requirement extracted from an RFP or tender document.

description.description: Full description of what is required
description.title: Description

priority.description: Priority level as stated or inferred from the RFP
priority.title: Priority

json_schema.title: RFPRequirement

rfp_qualification.CapabilityMatch.description: Assessment of how well company capabilities meet a single RFP requirement.

json_schema.description: Assessment of how well company capabilities meet a single RFP requirement.

match_level.description: How well the company meets this requirement
match_level.title: Match Level

evidence.description: Specific company capabilities, past projects, or certifications that support the match
evidence.title: Evidence

gap_description.description: Description of what is missing or weak if partial or no match, empty string if full match
gap_description.title: Gap Description

effort_to_close.description: Estimated effort to close the gap if any
effort_to_close.title: Effort To Close

json_schema.title: CapabilityMatch

rfp_qualification.QualificationMatrix.description: Aggregated go/no-go qualification assessment across all RFP requirements.

json_schema.description: Aggregated go/no-go qualification assessment across all RFP requirements.

go_recommendation.description: Whether the company should bid on this RFP
go_recommendation.title: Go Recommendation

not_matched.description: Number of requirements not matched at all
not_matched.title: Not Matched

critical_gaps.description: Summary of the most important unmet mandatory requirements
critical_gaps.title: Critical Gaps

key_strengths.description: Summary of the strongest competitive advantages for this bid
key_strengths.title: Key Strengths

win_probability.description: Estimated probability of winning the bid
win_probability.title: Win Probability

strategic_considerations.description: Strategic factors beyond requirement matching: relationship, market positioning, learning opportunity
strategic_considerations.title: Strategic Considerations

json_schema.title: QualificationMatrix
